name: Week 13 Pull — DELOAD (Spotlight: Bicep Curl + High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch)
description: **DELOAD WEEK.** Same deload as W13 push — W10-W12 were 3 hard weeks, fatigue signals are firing, the locked 3-hard/1-deload cadence puts the cut here. **HOLD all loads, CUT volume ~60-70%.** Spotlights rotate to the **curl + crunch finishers** (bicep curl, high cable curl, cable crunch) — the lightest lifts of the day, ideal deload spotlights. **Trap-3 raise replaces the behind-back shrug block** this week (planned rotation) — the shrugs gave a strong first signal W12D3 ('crackly pop, then everything aligned'); trap-3 raises isolate the lower trap even more directly at a lighter load. **W12D3 takeaways baked in:** pull-ups all-slow on straight bar 8/6/6/5 ('felt great for the first time in a long time'); chest-supported row 35 'good and easy'; DB row held form at 80; reverse fly 30° reverse-incline variant clicked; face pull cue change was transformative ('like another exercise, neck feels less engaged'); high cable curl ramped to 170.

dayOverview.theme: DELOAD — hold every load, cut every exercise a set. The curl + crunch finishers are the spotlight because they're the lightest work of the day; a deload is the right week to drill cues with nothing heavy competing. Trap-3 raise replaces the behind-back shrug block on the planned rotation — same trap-chain intent, lower-trap isolation, even lighter load. Watch the right-shoulder cluster: if it's worse than W13D1 push, that's the structural signal — PT eval.

spotlights.why: The bicep curl is the W13 spotlight (tracker says 'preacher curl' — incline curl is the de-facto lift here, preacher bench is always taken). W12D3 had BOTH sides weak with 8+2 assisted on set 3 — but the user flagged they skipped the left-first protocol and there was cumulative fatigue before it. A deload puts the curl on fresher legs: drill left-first, strict reps, full stretch + 2s pause. The reflection question: do the numbers recover when the fatigue ahead of them is cut?

W12D3 ramped to 170 ('approaching working weight; 180 would be hard but doable'). Deload HOLDS 170 — drill the upper-arm-parallel-and-fixed setup instead of chasing 180. The pec-stabilizes-the-arm sensation the user noted is positional and fine.

Held at 130 (W12D3 done without comment). Deload drills the cue that makes it an ab lift and not a hip-flexor lift: hips LOCKED, spine flexes, crunch from the abs only. Light, controlled, full attention.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown · W12 face pull + reverse fly · W13 bicep curl + high cable curl + cable crunch (current, DELOAD) · W14 cycle back to pull-up + DB row.
watchpoints: **This is a DELOAD — hold loads, cut sets, resist pushing.** Pull-ups stay 6-8 but 3 sets not 4. DB row drops to 2 sets at 70 only (skip the 60 and 80). Lat pulldown, face pull → 2 sets. If a lift feels easy, that's the deload working.

**Right shoulder symptom cluster — cross-check against W13D1 push.** If the line-of-sensation / grip-triggered pain is WORSE than it was on push day, that flips the working hypothesis from functional to structural — **book a PT eval, don't wait.** If it's the same or better, the deload + trap-chain work is doing its job.

**Trap-3 raise replaces the behind-back shrug block** (planned rotation). The shrugs got a strong W12D3 signal; trap-3 raises hit the lower trap even more directly. Start VERY light — 5-10lb. The lower trap is small and fatigues fast. Same report-back: does it relieve or aggravate the shoulder cluster?

**Left bicep weaker than right** (W10D3 + W11D3). W12D3 both sides were weak but the user skipped the left-first protocol. This week: **left first, every set.** Strict reps only — no assist past asymmetry.
**DB row holds the MIDDLE of the pyramid (70), not the top (80).** Skipping the heavy 80 set IS the deload for this lift — don't substitute it back in.
Cool-down stays — 5-min block, permanent every gym day.

exercises.notes: Scap activation. Squeeze shoulder blades together, soft elbow.
Spine warm — slow, full ROM each direction.
Active hang, not collapsed — engage lats, pull shoulders DOWN away from the ears even at full extension.

**LOAD:** Bodyweight. **DELOAD: 3 sets, NOT 4.** Reps stay 6-8 (W12D3's all-slow straight-bar 8/6/6/5 was clean — 'felt great for the first time in a long time').

**Straight bar is fine** — W12D3 the user chose it deliberately ('wanted to get basics working') and it worked well. Stick with straight bar, or mix grips if preferred; either way:
- Active hang start, not collapsed
- Pull elbows DOWN to ribs, NOT chin to bar
- Chest leads to the bar at the top
- Eyes ahead, head neutral — no chin tucking
- **Slow eccentric, ~2-3s**, return to an active hang — the all-slow tempo was the W12D3 win
- Stop the set when the rep stops being clean — never kip

**Right shoulder watch:** W11D3 the front of the right shoulder lit up on a wide grip. If it talks, drop to a close or neutral grip set.

sets.name: 🔆 Station 2: DB Rack / Bench Block (Bicep Curl Spotlight + Chest-Supported Row + DB Row + Reverse Fly — DELOAD)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 35lb (HOLD — W12D3 had 35 available and 'good and easy'). **DELOAD: 2 sets.** Hold 35 — do not push to 40 even though W12 said it had headroom; that's a W14 decision.
- **Chest stays GLUED to the bench** — if it lifts off, too heavy
- Pull the elbow toward the HIP, not the armpit
- Both elbows pull together — asymmetric = slow down
- 2s eccentric, 1s squeeze, no DB slam at the bottom

**LOAD:** 70lb (HOLD — the MIDDLE of the W12 pyramid). **DELOAD: 2 sets at 70 only — skip the 60 warmup set AND the 80 top set.** Dropping the heavy 80 is the deload for this lift; do not add it back.
- Chest-supported on the incline bench
- Pull the elbow toward the HIP, not the armpit
- Don't rotate the torso
- Slow 2s descent, 1s squeeze
- 70 should feel controlled and clean — W12D3 held form even at 80, so 70 on a deload is honest practice reps, not a grind

**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD). **DELOAD: 2 sets.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Sit on the incline bench (~45-60°)**, back against the bench, DBs hanging at the sides — this is the de-facto curl (preacher bench is always taken; preacher at 12.5/side is the swap if it's somehow free)
- **Left arm first, every set** — W10D3 + W11D3 showed the left weaker; W12D3 the user skipped left-first and both sides went weak. The weak side gets the freshest reps. This week, no excuse — the deload cut the fatigue ahead of this lift.
- **Full stretch at the bottom — pause 2s** (the user's own cue from W11D3)
- **Curl smoothly to a peak squeeze**, no swing
- **2-3s eccentric** — fight the long-head stretch
- **Stop each arm at strict failure** — do NOT assist past the asymmetry. A clean 8 beats 8+2 assisted.

**Reflection — the deload question:** W12D3 both sides struggled (8+2 assisted), possibly from cumulative fatigue. This week the volume ahead of the curl is cut. Do the numbers recover? If left hits a clean 8-10 today, fatigue-ordering was the W12 explanation. If it's still weak fresh, the curl placement may need to move earlier in W14.

**LOAD:** 20lb (HOLD — 22.5 'doesn't exist at this gym,' confirmed). **DELOAD: 2 sets.**
- **30° reverse-incline (chest-down on the incline bench)** — W12D3 locked this as the default form ('WAY better than the hunched-over version'). Set the bench to ~30°.
- DBs hanging fully extended at start, **soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED throughout**
- **Lead with the elbow, hand passive** — same pattern as the lateral raise
- **Wings out and slightly UP to shoulder height** — sweep, don't lift
- **2s eccentric, 1s squeeze** — no swing, no momentum

sets.notes: **One trip to the DB rack covers all 4 lifts.** Circuit or straight sets — your call. Bench at incline ~30° for the rows + incline curl AND the reverse fly (30° reverse-incline). 2 rounds, not 3 — this is the deload.

exercises.equipment: regular lat pulldown (default, 140) — dual pulley fallback 70/side ≈ 140 if the machine is occupied
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 140 (HOLD — W12D3 confirmed clean on the regular machine). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.**
- **Wide grip near the bend** — more lat isolation, less bicep
- **Slight backward lean ~15°, NO further** — more = it becomes a row
- **Pull the bar to the UPPER CHEST — NEVER behind the neck**
- 'Lats squeezing oranges into the back pockets' at the bottom
- 1s pause at the contraction, 2s controlled return to a full overhead stretch
- Knees pinned firmly under the pad

sets.name: Station 4: Cable Column — Face Pull (DELOAD — 2 Sets)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 42.5 (HOLD). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.** Keep the W12D3 cue protocol — that's the lift now, the user said it 'felt like another exercise' and 'neck feels less engaged.'
- **Pull the rope to face level** — not to the chest
- **ELBOWS HIGH (above the shoulders)** — the make-or-break cue; if they drop, the rear delt offloads
- **External rotate at the top — knuckles point UP, like a double-biceps pose.** This is why the lift exists.
- **1s peak hold** at the contracted position — count it
- **Slow controlled return, 2s** — let the cable pull the arms back

**Setup: pick the cable column you'll finish the workout on** — the curl + crunch finisher lives at the same column. Slide the rope to the high attachment after this.

sets.name: 🔆 Station 5: Cable Column Finisher — High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch (Spotlight, DELOAD)

exercises.equipment: cable column (high attachment) — same column as face pull
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 170 (HOLD — W12D3 actual working weight). **DELOAD: 1 working set per arm is the prescription** — treat the 2nd round as optional, stop at 1/arm if it feels complete. Do NOT push to 180; that's a post-deload decision.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the setup every rep:**
- **Single D-handle, one arm at a time** (the W12D3 setup that 'felt nice')
- **Stand sideways, arm out at shoulder level**
- **Upper arm parallel to the floor, FIXED** — if the elbow drops, it becomes an ordinary curl. The fixed upper arm is the entire point of the high-cable variant.
- **Curl the hand to the ear**, peak squeeze
- **Slow eccentric, no bounce at the start**
- The pec-stabilizes-the-arm sensation the user noted W12D3 is positional and normal — the upper arm parallel at shoulder level recruits the pec to hold position. Not a sign anything's wrong.

**Reflection:** with the upper arm truly fixed, does 170 feel like honest bicep work, or is the shoulder/pec doing the holding job? On a deload, get this dialed at 170 before W14 considers 180.

**LOAD:** cable column 130 (HOLD — W12D3 done without comment). **DELOAD: 2 sets.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the cue that makes this an ab lift:**
- **High attachment + rope** (swap from the D-handle — same column)
- **Kneel under the high cable**
- **HIPS LOCKED** — the single most important cue. The hips do not move.
- **Crunch from the abs ONLY** — the spine flexes, the hips stay put. Hips moving = hip-flexor work, not abs. If you feel it in the hip flexors, you've lost the cue.
- **Chest toward the thighs**, hold the crunch 1 second
- **Slow eccentric**

**Reflection:** at the bottom of each rep, is it the abs that flexed the spine, or did the hips fold? Locking the hips is the whole lift.

sets.notes: **The spotlight block — lightest work of the day, drilled deep.** High cable curl: 1 working set per arm (deload). Cable crunch: 2 sets. Same cable column as the face pull.

sets.name: 🆕 Station 6: Trap-3 Raise (Replaces Behind-Back Shrug Block — Planned Rotation)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 5-10lb DBs — start at 5. The lower trap is small and fatigues fast; 5-10lb is genuinely plenty. 3 sets.

**Why this lift, this week** (continues the W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion — see `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`):
- Behind-back shrugs got a strong signal W12D3 ('crackly pop, then everything aligned'). **Trap-3 raise rotates in as planned** — it isolates the lower trap ('trap 3') even more directly than the shrug, at a much lighter load. The lower trap is the underused side of the upper-trap-dominant pattern.
- The shrugs and trap-3 raises alternate across pull days — don't do both the same day.

**Cues (drill every rep):**
- **Lie face-down on an incline bench (~30-45°)**, a light DB in each hand
- **Arms hang toward the floor, thumbs pointing UP** (external rotation) — the thumbs-up is non-negotiable, it sets the lower-trap line
- **Lift the arms into a Y — at ~135° from the torso**, NOT straight overhead. Raise until the arms are in line with the ears or slightly above.
- **Lift from the SHOULDER BLADES, not the hands** — the cue is the scapula sliding down-and-in, the arms just go along for the ride
- **1-2s hold at the top**, slow eccentric back to neutral
- No momentum, no bouncing off the bottom

**What to watch (REPORT BACK):** does this relieve the right-shoulder cluster the way the W12D3 shrugs did? Same hypothesis test — relief = functional pattern (keep going), aggravation = structural (PT eval). Also note whether the lower trap fatigues fast — if 5lb is hard by rep 8, that IS the lower trap working (it should feel disproportionately weak vs. its size).

sets.notes: Closing trap-chain block at the incline bench. 3 sets, very light load, the cue is the whole lift. ~4 min including rest. Alternates with behind-back shrugs across pull days — trap-3 this week, shrugs return W14.

sets.name: Cool-Down — 5 Min Fascia/Recovery Block (Permanent Every-Gym-Day Closing Block)

exercises.notes: 30s each arm at MID elbow position. Pec minor lengthen — the antagonist to the upper-trap chain loaded all session.
Passive hang from the pull-up bar. **Don't engage** — let the shoulders unpack and the spine decompress.
30s each side on the mat. Draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended. Breathe into the rotated rib cage.
45s each side. Front leg + back leg at 90°. Sink the hips toward the floor — hip-rotator decompression.
60s slow breathing. Knees wide, arms extended forward. Breathe into the back ribs — parasympathetic shift before leaving the gym.

sets.notes: **5 minutes of recovery work, non-negotiable closing block (permanent since W12D3).** On a deload week this is the connective-tissue catch-up window — give it the full 5 minutes.

tips: **DELOAD WEEK — hold every load, cut every exercise a set.** Pull-ups 3 sets (not 4), DB row 2 sets at 70 only (skip 60 and 80), lat pulldown + face pull 2 sets, high cable curl 1 set/arm. If a lift feels easy, that's the design.
**Spotlight: the curl + crunch finishers** (bicep curl, high cable curl, cable crunch) — the lightest work of the day, drilled deep on a week with nothing heavy competing.

**Bicep curl: LEFT FIRST, every set, strict reps.** W12D3 the user skipped left-first and both sides went weak. The deload cut the fatigue ahead of the curl — the reflection question is whether the numbers recover.

**Trap-3 raise replaces the shrug block** (planned rotation). VERY light (5-10lb), thumbs up, lift from the shoulder blades, arms in a Y at 135°. Report whether it relieves or aggravates the right shoulder.
**Right shoulder cluster — cross-check vs. W13D1 push.** Worse than push day = structural signal, book a PT eval. Same or better = the deload + trap work is helping.
**Face pull holds 42.5 + the W12D3 cue protocol** — elbows high, external rotation, 1s hold. The cue is the lift, not the load.
**Cool-down stays — 5-min block, permanent every gym day.**
Rotation plan: W14 cycles back to pull-up + DB row; behind-back shrugs return W14 (alternating with trap-3 raise).
Rest 2.5 min pull-ups, 90s DB block, 2 min lat pulldown, 90s face pull, 60s finisher, 60s trap-3 block.
formPhilosophy: Deload week — hold loads, cut sets, let connective tissue catch up. The curl/crunch spotlight and the trap-3 raise are both light by nature, which makes this the right week to drill their cues with full attention. The shoulder-cluster cross-check against push day is the most important data point of the week. Building decades, not weeks.
